title: Metal Body Stampings
entry_mechanism: PSAP Track 2 — International stamping company JV at KAEC using Ma'aden automotive steel or POSCO supply
executive_summary:
Metal body stampings — pressed steel panels (door skins, hood, trunk lid, roof, fender, body-in-white structural components) — are the structural skeleton of every passenger vehicle. Phase II timing reflects the capital intensity: a stamping facility requires large hydraulic presses (2,000–5,000-tonne capacity), transfer dies, and material handling automation that is only justified by dedicated OEM programme demand at scale.

Saudi Arabia's Phase II automotive assembly programme creates the OEM demand anchor for stamping investment. The global metal stamping market for automotive is valued at $42 billion annually. POSCO's Saudi Arabia steel investment interest and Ma'aden's steel programme (longer term) create a materials supply chain foundation. A Saudi stamping facility serves Saudi OEM assembly initially and exports to GCC assembly programmes as Saudi automotive capacity grows.

companies.name: Gestamp
companies.rationale: Gestamp is Europe's largest automotive stamping company with international JV experience and active Gulf market interest.

companies.name: Tower International (Shiloh)
companies.rationale: Shiloh Industries produces automotive metal stampings and has international manufacturing partnerships.

companies.name: Magna Cosma International
companies.rationale: Magna Cosma is the world's largest automotive body structures manufacturer.

companies.name: POSCO Automotive Steel
companies.rationale: POSCO produces automotive steel and has Saudi Arabia investment interest. POSCO stamping technology available alongside steel supply.

companies.name: Schuler Group
companies.rationale: Schuler manufactures automotive stamping presses and can support Saudi facility design and equipment supply.

partners.name: KAEC Automotive Zone
partners.upgrade: Heavy stamping zone at KAEC with 5,000-tonne press infrastructure; dedicated power supply; Gestamp JV anchor tenant

partners.name: Saudi OEM Assembly Programmes
partners.upgrade: Saudi OEM body-in-white sourced from Saudi stamping facility; long-term stamping supply agreement; IATF 16949 qualification support

partners.name: POSCO / Ma'aden Steel (future)
partners.current: Steel import; Ma'aden steel programme planning
partners.upgrade: Saudi automotive steel supply to Saudi stamping facility; Ma'aden-POSCO JV as Phase II steel anchor; closed Saudi steel → stamping → assembly loop

partners.name: KFUPM Mechanical Engineering
partners.current: Manufacturing engineering; forming and metal processing
partners.upgrade: Automotive stamping engineering programme; die design and forming simulation research; Saudi stamping engineer pipeline
